tagline: Work with Linear issues, teams, projects, and users from the command line.

global_flags.name: connection
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Linear connector credential.

commands.summary: List Linear issues through the implemented ETL stream.

commands.notes: Requires a fixed GraphQL document with bounded variables and redacted JSON output; not implemented in this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Creates a visible issue in the configured Linear workspace.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval token, execute, and typed confirmation when sensitive or destructive.
commands.notes: Deferred until Linear GraphQL mutation write actions and sensitive/admin policy are implemented.

commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval token, execute, and typed confirmation when sensitive or destructive.
commands.notes: Deferred until Linear GraphQL mutation write actions and sensitive/admin policy are implemented.

commands.summary: Create a comment on a Linear issue.
commands.risk: Creates a visible comment in Linear.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval token, execute, and typed confirmation when sensitive or destructive.
commands.notes: Deferred until explicit GraphQL mutation actions exist; no arbitrary mutation body is accepted.

commands.summary: List Linear projects through the implemented ETL stream.

commands.notes: Requires a fixed GraphQL document with bounded variables and redacted JSON output; not implemented in this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Creates a visible project in Linear.
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval token, execute, and typed confirmation when sensitive or destructive.
commands.notes: Deferred until explicit GraphQL mutation actions exist; no arbitrary mutation body is accepted.

commands.summary: List Linear teams through the implemented ETL stream.

commands.notes: Requires a fixed GraphQL document with bounded variables and redacted JSON output; not implemented in this metadata slice.
Planned ETL candidate from the official GraphQL query surface; not part of the current four-stream baseline.
Planned ETL candidate from the official GraphQL query surface; not part of the current four-stream baseline.
Planned ETL candidate from the official GraphQL query surface; not part of the current four-stream baseline.

commands.summary: List Linear users through the implemented ETL stream.

commands.notes: Requires a fixed GraphQL document with bounded variables and redacted JSON output; not implemented in this metadata slice.

commands.risk: Creates an outbound integration endpoint and may expose workspace events to external systems.
commands.approval: Blocked by default pending sensitive/admin policy, explicit destination allow-listing, preview, approval token, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Administrative integration mutation; not exposed as an executable command in this connector surface.

commands.risk: Deletes an integration endpoint and can disrupt external workflows.
commands.approval: Blocked by default pending sensitive/admin policy, preview, approval token, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Destructive administrative mutation; not exposed as an executable command in this connector surface.

commands.summary: Invite a user to a Linear workspace.
commands.risk: Changes workspace membership and may grant access to private Linear data.
commands.approval: Blocked by default pending sensitive/admin policy, preview, approval token, and typed confirmation.
commands.notes: Administrative membership mutation; not exposed as an executable command in this connector surface.

commands.summary: Run an arbitrary Linear GraphQL operation.
commands.risk: A raw GraphQL surface could bypass stream/write review, redaction, and approval policy.
commands.approval: Disallowed. Use fixed reviewed stream, direct-read, or reverse-ETL actions instead.
commands.notes: Generic GraphQL query or mutation execution is intentionally not exposed.
Credentials are managed through Polymetrics credential commands and environment/stdin flows; secrets are never accepted in prompt text.
Connector configuration is handled by saved connections and runtime flags, not provider-specific config mutation commands.

help_topics.name: authentication
help_topics.summary: Use `api_key` or `access_token` through saved credentials or environment/stdin flows; never put secrets in command text.

help_topics.name: graphql-safety
help_topics.summary: Linear GraphQL operations are exposed only as reviewed fixed streams, direct reads, or reverse-ETL actions; raw arbitrary GraphQL is disallowed.

help_topics.name: writes
help_topics.summary: Linear mutations remain blocked until explicit write actions, previews, approvals, and sensitive/admin policy are implemented.
